segments.title: Anglo-Japanese alliance survival routes
segments.summary: The stream frames the Anglo-Japanese alliance surviving the 1920s around early First World War peace, U.S. isolationism, or dominion support from Australia and New Zealand.
segments.body: A viable survival path depends on a different U.S. or imperial political context, then has to be read against the treaty's China and Korea language.

evidence.note: The transcript lists three routes for the Anglo-Japanese alliance surviving: an early First World War end, U.S. isolationism after Harding, or Australia and New Zealand winning the imperial policy debate.
The main discussion begins by grounding the alliance in treaty language about China, Korea, mutual defense, and British and Japanese interests.
